crime: The block had formed around one girl — easy talk, shared jokes. She watched from the edge, as she has all her life.
That week, at the evening gathering, the girl saved her a seat and called on her to speak. The line came a half-beat wrong, the laugh never came, and the girl filled the silence kindly and moved on.
Every Monday the girl carried the block's discards to the incinerator room and loaded them before the furnace lit at three. She watched her do it for weeks.
The next Monday, she was already in the basement corridor when the girl went in with the load. The heavy iron door swung shut behind the girl.

As the girl turned back for the handle, she fixed the distance — the girl could not come within reach of the door, though it stood an arm's length ahead. At three the furnace took, and the small stone room filled with smoke and heat.
The girl was found after the firing, face down inside the incinerator room, one hand stretched toward the unlocked door — a clear step of untouched floor between her fingers and it.
A prisoner overcome by smoke normally gets to the door and falls against it. This one fell a full step short — an even margin of untouched floor before her.
That even margin means a line was held around a point out in the corridor — and the one prisoner who could set a distance no one else could close had stood there when the furnace took.
fields.epitaph: She was never quite in reach, and then she made sure of it.
fields.epithet: The Far Side of the Room
execution: A great ring of iron links lay around the stand in a rim channel, every coupling unseated. She stood in the circle's one gap, and the final span was brought around her chest — the two end-links coupled to each other before her, closing the ring with her inside its line.
A winch beneath the stand took up the ring. The slack left it join by join, each coupling seating true — the click walking the round toward her from both sides.
The last two end-links met before her chest and closed, iron to iron, around her. The circle was fast.
The winch kept taking up. The one span not riding the channel was the span around her; it tightened until the chest could no longer draw breath.

magic.name: the Set Distance
magic.text: She fixes a distance between herself and one chosen person; that person can never close it.
